name: Madrid
localTips: Lunch is the biggest meal of the day and many local restaurants serve a bargain 'menú del día' on weekdays — three courses, bread and a drink for a fraction of dinner prices.
Dinner doesn't really start until 9pm — arrive at a restaurant at 7pm and you'll likely be eating alone or find the kitchen still closed.
Validate your Metro or bus ticket and keep it until you exit — inspectors do random checks and fines apply even if you already tapped in.
Many major museums, including the Prado and Reina Sofía, offer free entry during the last one to two hours before closing on most days — check the current schedule online.
Tapas bars in La Latina and Malasaña are for hopping between, not settling into for hours — order one dish, one drink, then move to the next bar like locals do.

activities.title: Parque del Retiro & the Crystal Palace
activities.description: Madrid's grandest park, with rowboats on the lake, the glass-and-iron Palacio de Cristal reflecting in its pond, and wide avenues locals fill on weekends.

activities.title: Gran Vía architecture walk
activities.description: Madrid's 'Spanish Broadway', lined with early-20th-century towers like the Metropolis and Telefónica buildings, lit up spectacularly after dark.

activities.title: Gran Vía by night
activities.description: The same boulevard transforms after dark — neon theatre marquees, illuminated domes and a buzz that runs until the early hours.

activities.title: Plaza Mayor
activities.description: A grand 17th-century arcaded square that's hosted markets, bullfights and executions — now full of café terraces and street performers.

activities.title: Templo de Debod sunset
activities.description: A genuine 2,200-year-old Egyptian temple gifted to Spain, reflected in its pool at the edge of a hilltop park — one of the best free sunset spots in the city.

activities.title: Mercado de San Miguel browsing
activities.description: A restored 1916 iron-and-glass market hall packed with tapas stalls, jamón counters and cava bars — free to wander, pay only for what you sample.

activities.title: Mercado de San Antón
activities.description: A three-storey Chueca market with a rooftop terrace bar on top, gourmet stalls in the middle and fresh produce below — browse free, eat if something tempts you.

activities.title: Royal Palace exterior & Sabatini Gardens
activities.description: Europe's largest royal palace by floor area — the formal Sabatini Gardens out front are free and give the best photo angle of its endless baroque facade.

activities.title: Malasaña street wander
activities.description: Madrid's hipster heart — independent record shops, vintage stores and street art along narrow 19th-century streets that were once the centre of the 1980s Movida.

activities.title: Matadero Madrid cultural centre
activities.description: A former 1920s slaughterhouse turned free contemporary arts complex — galleries, a design library and a riverside terrace, most exhibitions free to enter.

activities.title: El Rastro Sunday flea market
activities.description: Madrid's biggest and oldest flea market floods La Latina's sloping streets every Sunday morning with antiques, leather goods and secondhand everything.

activities.title: Plaza de España
activities.description: A monumental square honouring Cervantes and Don Quixote, recently redesigned with a huge reflecting pool and green space at the foot of two 1950s towers.

activities.title: Calle de Alcalá & Puerta de Alcalá
activities.description: A freestanding neoclassical arch commissioned by Charles III in 1778 to mark the city's eastern gate, now marooned in traffic at the edge of Retiro park.

activities.title: Free evening hours at the Prado
activities.description: One of the world's great art museums — Velázquez's 'Las Meninas', Goya's 'Black Paintings' and Bosch's 'Garden of Earthly Delights' — free every evening for the last two hours.
activities.insiderNote: Free entry runs roughly 6–8pm on weekdays and 5–7pm Sundays; queue at the Puerta de los Jerónimos at least 30 minutes early, it moves fast once doors open.

activities.title: Free hours at Reina Sofía
activities.description: Home to Picasso's 'Guernica' — the museum offers free entry most afternoons and evenings, letting you see one of the 20th century's defining paintings at no cost.
activities.insiderNote: Free windows run roughly 7–9pm Monday, Wednesday to Saturday, and 1:30–7pm Sunday — always double-check current hours before you go, as they shift seasonally.

activities.title: Churros con chocolate at San Ginés
activities.description: A 24-hour chocolatería behind Puerta del Sol since 1894, serving thick hot chocolate and fried churros to everyone from breakfasting tourists to clubbers at 5am.
activities.insiderNote: The true local move is to go after a night out, not before bed — it's Madrid's unofficial last stop of the night.

activities.title: Tapas bar hopping in La Latina
activities.description: Calle Cava Baja is lined wall-to-wall with century-old tabernas — order one plate and one caña per stop, then move on, the way locals actually eat here.
activities.insiderNote: Sunday evening after El Rastro is when the street is busiest with locals, not tourists — go early on other nights to actually get a spot at the bar.

activities.title: Círculo de Bellas Artes rooftop
activities.description: A private arts society's rooftop terrace opens to anyone with a small day membership — one of the best 360° views over the city centre, especially at sunset.
activities.insiderNote: The small entry fee (a few euros) doubles as access to the building's exhibitions and cinema — arrive an hour before sunset for a table.

activities.title: Vermouth hour at a classic vermutería
activities.description: Sunday before lunch is vermouth o'clock in Madrid — draft vermut poured from the barrel at old-school spots like Casa Camacho, with olives and boquerones on the side.
activities.insiderNote: Ask for 'vermut de grifo' (draft vermouth) rather than a bottled brand — it's the local's order and noticeably cheaper.

activities.title: Mercado de Motores vintage market
activities.description: A monthly vintage and design market held inside the old Estación de las Delicias railway sheds — a locals' favourite far outside the tourist circuit.
activities.insiderNote: It only runs one weekend a month — check the current calendar online before planning around it.

activities.title: Flamenco peña in Lavapiés
activities.description: Skip the tourist tablaos for a peña — a members' club where flamenco is performed for its own community, sometimes announced only hours ahead.
activities.insiderNote: Ask staff at a local guitar shop or the Lavapiés cultural centre about that week's peña — it's word-of-mouth, not listed on ticket sites.

activities.title: Museo del Prado full entry
activities.description: Book ahead to skip the queue for Velázquez, Goya and El Greco in one of the world's finest painting collections, without waiting for the free evening window.
activities.bookingQuery: Museo del Prado skip the line ticket Madrid

activities.title: Reina Sofía timed entry
activities.description: Guaranteed access to Picasso's 'Guernica' and Spain's leading modern art collection without waiting for the free-hours queue.
activities.bookingQuery: Reina Sofia Guernica timed entry ticket Madrid

activities.title: Thyssen-Bornemisza Museum entry
activities.description: The third point of Madrid's 'Golden Triangle' of art — a private collection spanning seven centuries from medieval altarpieces to Picasso and Pollock.

activities.title: Royal Palace interior tour
activities.description: Walk through the Throne Room, the Royal Armoury and the ornate state apartments of Europe's largest functioning royal palace by floor area.
activities.bookingQuery: Royal Palace of Madrid interior ticket

activities.title: Santiago Bernabéu stadium tour
activities.description: Walk pitchside, through the changing rooms and trophy room of Real Madrid's home ground — essential even for casual football fans.

activities.title: Las Ventas bullring museum tour
activities.description: A guided walk through Spain's most important bullring, its Mudéjar-brick architecture and the small museum of matador history inside.
activities.bookingQuery: Las Ventas bullring museum tour Madrid

activities.title: Flamenco show at a tablao
activities.description: A high-energy live flamenco performance with guitar, cante and percussive dance in an intimate tablao — Corral de la Morería is the most storied address.

activities.title: Evening tapas & wine tasting tour
activities.description: A guided crawl through La Latina's tabernas with a local host, tasting jamón, tortilla and regional wines while hearing the stories behind each bar.
activities.bookingQuery: tapas and wine tasting night tour Madrid

activities.title: Toledo day trip
activities.description: A former imperial capital 30 minutes away by high-speed train, its walled old town stacking mosques, synagogues and a Gothic cathedral within one skyline.

activities.title: Segovia day trip
activities.description: A Roman aqueduct still standing after 2,000 years, a fairy-tale castle said to have inspired Disney, and Castile's best roast suckling pig, an hour from Madrid.
